recentHighlights: Doctor Bashir, I Presume? (S5E16) — Bashir's genetic engineering reveal: his parents had him illegally resequenced at age seven, his entire brilliance manufactured. Zimmerman arrives to model the LMH after Bashir; the secret emerges through a hologram. Richard takes a two-year sentence to protect Julian's career. Rom tells Leeta he loves her. O'Brien's friendship survives the lie: "From now on... you play from back here. I'll play from up there." Written by Ronald D. Moore, directed by Livingston.

A Simple Investigation (S5E17) — Rene Echevarria's Odo noir. An Orion Syndicate operative named Arissa comes to the station; Odo falls into genuine intimacy — his first sexual experience with a humanoid. The bittersweet reveal: her memories were wiped by Idanian Intelligence; the woman he loved was a constructed identity. She recovers her memories, reveals she has a husband, and leaves. The question: is the person you loved who she was during the suppression the "real" her, or did you love a fabrication?

By Inferno's Light (S5E15) — The Dominion War's second punch: Cardassia joins the Dominion, Dukat disowns Ziyal, Garak conquers his claustrophobia in a crawlspace to finish Tain's work. The Changeling Bashir nearly destroys the Bajoran sun. The false-invasion trap — warp signatures that don't correspond to real ships. Martok re-born, alliances rebuilt. Written by Behr & Wolfe, directed by Landau.

For the Uniform (S5E13) — Sisko threatens to poison a Maquis world — and does it. The bluff is indistinguishable from the real thing. Eddington surrenders not because he's trapped but because the script demands it: Valjean must sacrifice himself for Javert. Written by Peter Allan Fields, directed by Lobl.
